comments.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
comments.text:
✨Tuvalu is a nation fighting for its future against the rising tides. If you found this look into their lives moving, there are so many more incredible stories waiting for you. 
Discover more breathtaking travel documentaries from around the world at this Playlist: 
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gaRWfjWqAvs&list=PLPsFFj_F_V2ESDcWWCFwfHwlojUqv2I8E&index=1

comments.author: @ThompsonRyan-u4h
comments.text: i really living in tuvalu a day

comments.author: @PlanetVibeOfficial01
comments.text: A beautiful country, I am very impressed by its geography. Will it still exist in 30 years? With climate change becoming increasingly severe.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for your kind words and for watching! It’s a tough question that many scientists are debating. While the threat of rising sea levels is very real, Tuvalu is fighting back through land reclamation projects and international climate advocacy. Whether they can stay above water in 30 years depends heavily on how the world acts today. Let's hope for the best for this beautiful nation!

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@TommyJoseph-l8g 
Thank you. It’s a heartbreaking reality, but sharing their story is the first step toward raising awareness. ❤️

comments.author: @TaomiaF
comments.text: Thank you for sharing this touching and meaningful story about Tuvalu.  It shows the life in an Big Ocean State, its resilience and the joy of living in a Big Ocean State.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@TaomiaF
Thank you so much for your thoughtful comment! I’m glad you appreciated the focus on their resilience and the beauty of life in a Big Ocean State. It was truly an honor to share their story. Thanks for watching!

comments.author: @LologaGerega
comments.text:
Watched from Papua New Guinea 🇵🇬. Thank you so much for showing the beautiful people in their own beautiful God blessed and given land. I feel for them. I am sure God will provide them a place to resettle. 
For preservation of their uniqueness they need to document their - language, cultural activities and stories and etc. God bless Tuvalu.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@LologaGerega
Thank you so much for watching from Papua New Guinea! I completely agree—preserving their unique culture, language, and stories is vital as they face these environmental challenges. That is exactly why I wanted to share their journey through this documentary. Sending love and blessings to both PNG and Tuvalu!

comments.author: @ManuiaToitoona-r4y
comments.text: Thanks for the update on our beautiful ❤️ island of Tuvalu, That's where you can see the Miracle of God 💗. 🌺💐🌹

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@ManuiaToitoona-r4y
You’re very welcome! It was a true honor to visit and document your beautiful home. Tuvalu is indeed a special place with a spirit like nowhere else. Thank you for watching and for your kind words!

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@nabarundas6143 
Thank you! It truly is a stunning paradise, which makes the 'sinking' reality even more heartbreaking. Glad you enjoyed the views!

replies.author: @markanthonyyap6900
replies.text: Where did the other boy come from?😅

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@markanthonyyap6900 
Thanks for your comment! That specific moment highlights just how unique and complex the situation in Tuvalu really is. It’s a part of their daily life that many find hard to believe!

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: The beauty of the island and the resilience of its people are truly inspiring. Thank you for sharing such a meaningful story with the world.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: It’s truly a privilege to share such a unique story, and we're so glad it resonated with you. ❤️

comments.author: @AlusMarkmaliwa-k3k
comments.text: I can see a lady wearing a meri blaus with png flags on it.. beautiful people there..

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@AlusMarkmaliwa-k3k
Great eye for detail! It’s wonderful to see these cultural connections across the Pacific islands. The people of Tuvalu are indeed incredibly kind and welcoming.

comments.author: @ludekmeduna5986
comments.text: Why there is a humming bird drinking nectar from a flower ? They don't live in Tuvalu  (7:43)

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@ludekmeduna5986 
Thank you for your keen eye and for pointing that out! We truly appreciate the correction and will be more careful with geographical accuracy in our future documentaries. Thanks for watching The Planet Journal!

comments.author: @TruePlanetOffical
comments.text:
Wow this was actually really cool. Had no clue Tuvalu was like that. The airport runway turning into the soccer field every day is wild 😂. People there seem so chill and tight-knit, kinda makes you jealous of that community vibe. The whole sinking island thing is heavy though… really makes you think.
Solid video man, learned a lot.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for watching and for your thoughtful perspective on the unique community spirit and the environmental challenges facing Tuvalu!

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@NomadicCorner 
It truly does! There is a beautiful simplicity and calmness to life in Tuvalu that is hard to find anywhere else. Thank you for watching and sharing your thoughts!

comments.author: @benjaminmwaria238
comments.text: Something need to be done now to preserve this place. It is heaven on earth.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@benjaminmwaria238 
Thank you for your comment. I couldn't agree more. Tuvalu is truly breathtaking, but its future depends on urgent global action. Raising awareness through stories like this is our first step toward preserving this paradise.

comments.author: @ChanelNatureTravel4K
comments.text: It's hard to imagine living on islands this remote.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: It truly is. The extreme isolation shapes such a unique and resilient way of life. Thanks for watching and sharing your thoughts!

comments.author: @rswpt
comments.text: this is heaven on earth, we gotta act now and fast to change things around and reverse climate change as as soon as possible to save paradises like this, i love my planet, it is so precious..we must save it

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@rswpt 
Thank you so much for your heartfelt comment! Tuvalu is indeed a breathtaking paradise that reminds us what’s at stake. We hope this documentary helps spark the awareness needed to protect our planet before it’s too late. Let’s keep sharing the message!
